findings.summary: Legacy wire-to-pin connectivity completely broken: all 32 RJ45 data pins appear as isolated unnamed nets instead of being connected to SIG1-SIG8 labeled nets

correct.description: 6 components correctly extracted (2 caps, 4 RJ45 connectors) with correct values, footprints, and MPNs from legacy custom fields
GND correctly identified as only power rail
BOM correctly extracts MPN (C315C221K1G5TA, RJHSE5080) and manufacturer from legacy Field entries

incorrect.description: 32 RJ45 data pins appear as isolated __unnamed_0 through __unnamed_31 single-pin nets. All should be on SIG1-SIG8. SIG1-SIG3,SIG6 have zero pins resolved. SIG4,SIG5,SIG7,SIG8 each only have 1 cap pin (missing 3 RJ45 pins each).
Reports 41 nets but actual design has only 9 (GND + SIG1-SIG8). Inflated by 32 spurious single-pin nets from broken wire tracing.

suggestions: This is a minimal repro case for KH-016: only 6 components, 8 signal wires, and pure wire-based connectivity. Good test fixture for debugging the legacy wire tracer.

findings.summary: PCB analysis correct overall but copper_layers_used=0 despite tracks on both Front and Back, and front_side/back_side component counts are 0 — RESOLVED: now reports copper_layers_used=2, front_side=6

correct.description: All 6 real components + 1 logo correctly identified with correct net assignments
Track layer distribution correct: Front:130, Back:74. 204 total segments, 4 vias.

incorrect.description: copper_layers_used=0 but tracks exist on both Front and Back copper layers
front_side=0 and back_side=0 but all 6 components are on Front

suggestions: copper_layers_used should count layers from track.layer_distribution
front_side/back_side should count footprints by their layer
